data.title: Gold falls for the week as U.S. delays decision on Israel-Iran war involvement
data.description:
Gold futures fell Friday to close the holiday-shortened week lower after President Trump backtracked from launching an attack on Iran that had looked imminent a few days ago, with the White House saying the president will decide in the next two weeks whether the U.S. will get involved in the Israel-Iran air war.

Analysts said the shift in Trump's tone reduced fears of imminent action that could escalate hostilities, taking the edge off safe-haven demand for gold.

The bigger news for gold this week may have been the U.S. Federal Reserve's decision to hold interest rates steady as expected, but while policymakers still expect to cut rates by half a percentage point this year, they slightly slowed the pace to a single quarter-percentage-point cut in each of 2026 and 2027.

If the Fed finds it tough to lower borrowing costs, it would be negative for non-yielding gold, which performs better in a lower-rate environment.

Gold has failed to benefit from heightened geopolitical tensions, with the U.S. dollar emerging as the market's preferred safe-haven for now, Tradu.com's Nikos Tzabouras said in a note, adding the yellow metal's 28% YTD rally may be showing fatigue as the Fed looks reluctant to cut interest rates, thus benefiting the dollar.

But over the longer term, Tzabouras thinks gold likely will remain the preferred risk-off safe-haven asset as the de-dollarization trend continues over the longer term, and that the path to fresh all-time highs is still open.

Wall Street banks have diverged over whether gold can continue its record-setting rally, with Goldman Sachs recently reaffirming its forecast of $4,000/oz gold by next year, while Citigroup said prices likely will dip below $3K in 2026.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery finished -1.8% on the week to $3,368.10/oz, its lowest settlement value since June 11, while front-month June Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -0.8% to $35.976/oz this week, its lowest close since June 5; on Friday, gold and silver fell 0.6% and 2.4%, respectively.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Incyte says FDA delays target action date for ruxolitinib cream
data.description:
• Incyte (NASDAQ:INCY) said on Friday that the U.S. FDA has extended the review period for the label expansion application of its janus kinase inhibitor, ruxolitinib cream, for the treatment of children 2–11 years old with atopic dermatitis.
  • The target action date has been extended by three months to September 19, 2025, the company said.
  • “The FDA extended the PDUFA action date to allow time to review additional chemistry, manufacturing and controls data on the 0.75% strength submitted by Incyte in response to a recent FDA information request,” Incyte added.
  • Shares of the company dropped 2% after the bell.

data.title: NRx Pharmaceuticals files $150M mixed shelf offering
data.description:
• NRx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:NRXP) has filed a shelf registration to offer up to $150M in securities over time.
  • Securities may include common stock, preferred stock, debt securities, depositary shares, warrants, rights, purchase contracts, or units.
  • NRXP shares up 2.2% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: SEC filing
